# UC-17.1.61 · ISE Multi-Site Topology — Cross-Site Replication Lag and WAN Loss Impact

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE has copies in different cities. When the connection between cities is bad, the copies can't keep up with each other. We watch both the ISE-side and the network-side, so we can tell which side is at fault.*

---

## Description

Correlates ISE cross-site replication lag with WAN-link instability — distinguishes ISE-internal issues from WAN-induced lag.

## Value

Multi-site ISE deployments are sensitive to WAN quality. When lag spikes, knowing whether it's ISE-internal or WAN-driven changes the recovery path. This UC provides that distinction.

## Implementation

Schedule every 15 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Drop WAN to a lab remote site. ISE replication lag spikes; UC-17.1.61 fires showing the region with WAN flaps + lag.

### Negative scenario

WAN restored. Both metrics return to baseline.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Multi-site ISE deployment.
- Network TA collecting WAN events.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection.
### Step 2 — Search.
### Step 3 — Validate.
### Step 4 — Dashboard combining lag and WAN.
### Step 5 — Troubleshooting: prioritise WAN over ISE if both correlate.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" "ReplicationStatus")
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:ios" "%LINEPROTO-5")
| eval region = coalesce(region, "unknown")
| stats max(LastSyncDelta) as max_lag_s, count(eval(like(_raw, "%LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN%"))) as wan_flaps by region
| where max_lag_s > 600 OR wan_flaps > 2
```

## Visualization

(1) Per-region lag timechart. (2) Per-region WAN-flap timeline. (3) Correlation panel: lag vs flaps.

## Known False Positives

- **Scheduled WAN maintenance** — cross-reference change calendar.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — Distributed Deployment](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
